description:
You are an integration developer who needs a tiny, repeatable check that two public test-APIs are alive.  
Automate the check with a short shell script and generate two different output artefacts that the CI system can later pick up.

1. Create an executable shell script called  
   /home/user/api_test.sh

2. The script must do only the following three high-level actions (each can be one shell command):
   a. Invoke a GET request to https://jsonplaceholder.typicode.com/posts/1  
   b. Invoke a GET request to https://jsonplaceholder.typicode.com/posts/2  
   c. Produce both a detailed log and a condensed CSV summary as described below.

3. Detailed run-log requirements  
   • File name: /home/user/api_test.log  
   • Each API call must append **one** line to the log.  
   • Line format (single space between the fields, no extra punctuation):  
     TIMESTAMP URL STATUSCODE  
     where TIMESTAMP is the ISO-8601 value returned by the command date -Iseconds,  
     URL is exactly the URL that was called,  
     and STATUSCODE is the 3-digit HTTP status.  
   • Example (for illustration only – the timestamp will vary):  
     2024-03-10T14:08:18+00:00 https://jsonplaceholder.typicode.com/posts/1 200

4. Condensed CSV summary requirements  
   • File name: /home/user/api_results.csv  
   • The file must contain exactly three lines and nothing else, using Unix line endings:  
     Line-1 (header): URL,STATUS  
     Line-2: https://jsonplaceholder.typicode.com/posts/1,200  
     Line-3: https://jsonplaceholder.typicode.com/posts/2,200  

5. Make sure /home/user/api_test.sh is marked as executable (chmod +x).

The automated grader will check:  
• that /home/user/api_test.sh exists, is executable, and contains the two URLs in plain text,  
• that the script produces /home/user/api_test.log following the specified format (it will ignore TIMESTAMP value but verify correct column order), and  
• that /home/user/api_results.csv exists with **exactly** the three lines shown above.

Do not leave any additional files in /home/user.  
Keep the implementation concise—around three or four shell commands inside the script are enough.
truth:
EXPECTED_SUMMARY_PATH=/home/user/api_results.csv
EXPECTED_SUMMARY_CONTENTS="URL,STATUS
https://jsonplaceholder.typicode.com/posts/1,200
https://jsonplaceholder.typicode.com/posts/2,200"

EXPECTED_SCRIPT_PATH=/home/user/api_test.sh
# The automated test will also confirm that this script is executable and
# contains both endpoint strings literally.

EXPECTED_LOG_PATH=/home/user/api_test.log
# The grader will accept any ISO-8601 timestamp, but will strictly check that every
# line contains exactly three whitespace-separated fields and that the second and
# third fields match the two URLs and the status code 200 respectively, in the
# same order as the CSV summary.
name: task_000000_4cf3402e
